evidence.heading: Global experience, in numbers
evidence.intro: Public data on self-ordering in restaurants. Market benchmarks — not kiyo results.

technomic.text: Technomic research: the average kiosk order is about 8% higher in QSR and 15% higher in fast casual.

shakeshack.text: Shake Shack's annual report: kiosks help serve more guests at peak — fewer lines, less waiting, less friction.

arcos.text: Arcos Dorados, the largest McDonald's franchisee: app, delivery and kiosks together drove 61% of systemwide sales in 2025.

mcdonalds.text: McDonald's 2022 report: digital channels in its six largest markets — nearly 35% of sales, over $25 billion.

evidence.disclaimer: Some figures combine several digital channels, not kiosks alone. Outcomes depend on location, menu, traffic and implementation quality — this is not a guarantee.

service.heading: Everything included
service.intro: One agreement — the whole stack, from hardware to support.

faq.heading: Frequently asked questions
faq.intro: The essentials, briefly. The rest — in person.

q1.q: Which businesses is the kiosk a good fit for?
q1.a: Fast food, coffee shops, bakeries, food courts and any venue where guests order, pay and collect themselves.

q2.a: Yes — it's the core of the solution: kiosk orders land in your iiko workflow, menus and stop-lists stay in sync. The configuration is confirmed during the assessment.

q3.a: Click, Uzum, Payme, HUMO, Uzcard, Visa and Mastercard. Acceptance is configured per merchant.

q4.q: Can we rent the hardware instead of buying it?
q4.a: Yes. Rental with a low initial payment is available — you can start without purchasing the hardware. Terms are calculated individually.

q5.q: Can the kiosk match our brand?
q5.a: Yes: logo, colors and menu presentation are configured so the kiosk looks like part of your venue.

q6.q: How many languages does the interface support?
q6.a: Uzbek, Russian and English as standard. Additional languages can be configured for your guests.

q7.a: Yes. Categories, photos, modifiers, combos and iiko synchronization are part of the implementation.

q8.a: kiyo: delivery, mounting, connection and end-to-end testing at your venue.

q9.a: Yes — before launch we train the team: helping guests, daily care, whom to contact for support.

q10.a: Maintenance, warranty and technical support are part of the offer. The exact service format is agreed individually.

q11.a: Yes: multiple locations, centralized menus, and a branch-by-branch rollout plan defined at the assessment stage.

q12.q: Can we add our own integration?
q12.a: Yes — loyalty, delivery and internal systems are discussed after a technical review of the scope.

q13.a: It depends on the number of kiosks, locations, the model (rental or purchase) and the setup scope. There's no public price list — we prepare an individual proposal after a short assessment.

q14.a: Leave your number or write on Telegram. A few questions about your format and iiko — and we'll prepare a proposal.

cta.heading: Shall we see how it would work for you?
cta.text: Leave your number — we'll call back, show a demo and prepare a tailored proposal.

productPage.title: A kiosk that looks like part of your venue
productPage.lead: A white body, a black display bezel and an interface in your brand. Guests notice the short queue — not the technology.

design.title: Hardware designed for dining rooms
design.text: The kiosk is built around a large portrait touchscreen in a slim black bezel on a white body. The payment terminal sits at a natural hand height next to the screen.
design.p3: Payment terminal at a comfortable height
design.p4: Floor-standing or counter placement — per venue

interface.title: An interface guests understand in seconds
interface.text: Large photos, clear categories, obvious buttons. Modifiers, combos and suggestions are built into the flow — in the guest's language.
interface.p1: Your logo, colors and menu presentation
interface.p2: Uzbek, Russian and English as standard

service.title: Delivered, installed and supported
service.text: kiyo delivers the kiosk, installs it at your venue, connects iiko and payments, trains the team and stays available. Rental means you can start without buying the hardware.

privacyPage.title: Privacy notice
privacyPage.intro: This notice explains what happens to the information you submit through this website and how analytics and cookies are used.

s1.title: What we collect
s1.text: When you submit the contact form, we collect the data you enter: your name, phone number, business name (optional) and message (optional). Technical context is stored alongside it: the page you submitted from, the referrer, UTM parameters, the site language and the submission time.

s2.title: Why we collect it
s2.text: For one purpose: to contact you about self-service kiosks — to call you back, answer questions and prepare a proposal. We do not sell or share this data with third parties for marketing.

s3.title: Where it is stored
s3.text: Submissions are stored in a private Google Sheets document operated by the company. Data is processed in Google's infrastructure and may be stored on servers outside Uzbekistan.

s4.title: Who can access it
s4.text: Only staff who handle sales and implementation have access, granted individually through Google accounts.

s5.title: Correction and deletion
s5.text: You may request to see, correct or delete your submitted data at any time — contact us through any channel on the contact page.

s6.title: Analytics
s6.text: The site may use Vercel Analytics, Vercel Speed Insights, Google Analytics 4 and Yandex Metrica for aggregate usage statistics. Analytics events never include the content of your submissions, your phone number or your name.

s7.title: Cookies
s7.text: The site uses a cookie to remember your language choice (NEXT_LOCALE). Analytics services, when enabled, may set their own cookies according to their policies.

s8.title: Legal entity
s8.text: The final legal entity, registration details and controller contacts will be published here before commercial launch. This notice must be reviewed by legal counsel before public use.

devWarning.missingContacts: Developer note: contact channels are not configured (NEXT_PUBLIC_TELEGRAM_URL / NEXT_PUBLIC_CONTACT_PHONE / NEXT_PUBLIC_CONTACT_EMAIL). This notice is hidden in production.
